title: Reviewing Your Day with God
subtitle: A Prayer of Reflection
description: This is a simple way of looking back over your day (or your week) with God. It helps you notice where God has been present, where you felt most alive, and where you might need His grace. Five short steps. No experience required.

steps.title: Start with Thanks
steps.subtitle: What are you grateful for?
steps.instruction: Get quiet. Take a few slow breaths. Ask God to bring to mind the gifts of today—big and small. A conversation. A meal. A moment of beauty. Something kind someone did for you. Let the gratitude come on its own.
steps.prompt: What’s one thing from today that you’re genuinely thankful for?

steps.title: Walk Through It
steps.subtitle: Replay your day
steps.instruction: Ask the Holy Spirit to guide your memory. Walk through your day from the time you woke up until right now, like you’re watching it on a screen. Don’t judge anything yet—just replay it. Where did you feel most alive? Where did you feel drained or off?
steps.prompt: As you walk through your day, what moments stand out?

steps.title: Name What You Felt
steps.subtitle: Pay attention to your emotions
steps.instruction: As you review, notice the emotions that came up. What brought you energy, joy, or peace? What left you anxious, frustrated, or sad? Your feelings aren’t random—they’re often clues to where God is working or where you need Him.
steps.prompt: What emotions surfaced today? What might they be telling you?

steps.title: Bring One Thing to God
steps.subtitle: Pick a moment and pray about it
steps.instruction: From everything you’ve noticed, choose one moment—good or hard—that stands out most. Bring it to God in prayer. If it was life-giving, thank Him. If it was painful, ask for healing or wisdom. Just talk to Him honestly about it.
steps.prompt: What’s the one thing you most want to talk to God about from today?

steps.title: Look Ahead
steps.subtitle: Give God tomorrow
steps.instruction: Shift your attention to tomorrow. What are you looking forward to? What are you dreading? Ask God to go ahead of you. You don’t need to have it all figured out—you just need to know He’ll be there.
steps.prompt: What do you need from God for tomorrow?
steps.scripture: The Lord himself goes before you and will be with you; he will never leave you nor forsake you. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged.
